For example, you may want your application to incorporate the capability to speak its dialog box messages to the user. A word-processing application might use the Speech Synthesis Manager to implement a command that speaks a selected section of a document to the user. Because sound samples can take up large amounts of room on disk, using text in place of sampled sound is extremely efficient. For example, a multimedia application might use the Speech Synthesis Manager to provide a narration of a QuickTime movie instead of including sampled-sound data on a movie track.

For example, a multimedia application might use the Speech Synthesis Manager to provide a narration of a QuickTime movie instead of including sampled-sound data on a movie track.

OS X v10.5 introduces native support for performing speech synthesis tasks using Core Foundation-based objects, such as speaking text represented as CFString objects and managing speech channel properties using a CFDictionary-based property dictionary. You should begin using these Core Foundation-based programming interfaces as soon as itâ€™s convenient, because future synthesizers will accept Core Foundation strings and data structures directly through the speech synthesis framework. # Speech Synthesis Manager | Apple Developer Documentation

- [ Application Services ](/documentation/applicationservices)

- [ Speech Synthesis Manager ](/documentation/applicationservices/speech_synthesis_manager)

-  Speech Synthesis Manager 

API Collection# Speech Synthesis Manager

## [Overview](/documentation/applicationservices/speech_synthesis_manager#overview)

The Speech Synthesis Manager, formerly called the Speech Manager, is the part of macOS that provides a standardized method for Mac apps to generate synthesized speech. For example, you may want your application to incorporate the capability to speak its dialog box messages to the user. A word-processing application might use the Speech Synthesis Manager to implement a command that speaks a selected section of a document to the user. Because sound samples can take up large amounts of room on disk, using text in place of sampled sound is extremely efficient. For example, a multimedia application might use the Speech Synthesis Manager to provide a narration of a QuickTime movie instead of including sampled-sound data on a movie track.

OS X v10.5 introduces native support for performing speech synthesis tasks using Core Foundation-based objects, such as speaking text represented as `CFString` objects and managing speech channel properties using a `CFDictionary`-based property dictionary. You should begin using these Core Foundation-based programming interfaces as soon as itâ€™s convenient, because future synthesizers will accept Core Foundation strings and data structures directly through the speech synthesis framework. In the meantime, existing buffer-based clients and synthesizers will continue to work as before, with strings and other data structures getting automatically converted as necessary.

### [Gestalt Constants](/documentation/applicationservices/speech_synthesis_manager#1679841)

You can check for version and feature availability information by using the Speech Synthesis Manager selectors defined in the [Gestalt Manager](/documentation/coreservices/carbon_core/gestalt_manager).

## [Topics](/documentation/applicationservices/speech_synthesis_manager#topics)

### [Changing Speech Attributes](/documentation/applicationservices/speech_synthesis_manager#1657450)

[`func CopySpeechProperty(SpeechChannel, CFString, UnsafeMutablePointer<CFTypeRef?>) -> OSErr`](/documentation/applicationservices/1459075-copyspeechproperty)Gets the value associated with the specified property of a speech channel.Deprecated[`func GetSpeechPitch(SpeechChannel, UnsafeMutablePointer<Fixed>) -> OSErr`](/documentation/applicationservices/1464774-getspeechpitch)Gets a speech channelâ€™s current speech pitch.Deprecated[`func GetSpeechRate(SpeechChannel, UnsafeMutablePointer<Fixed>) -> OSErr`](/documentation/applicationservices/1460797-getspeechrate)Gets a speech channelâ€™s current speech rate.Deprecated[`func SpeechBusy() -> Int16`](/documentation/applicationservices/1464581-speechbusy)Determines whether any channels of speech are currentlysynthesizing speech.Deprecated[`func SpeechBusySystemWide() -> Int16`](/documentation/applicationservices/1460113-speechbusysystemwide)Determines if any speech is currently being synthesizedin your application or elsewhere on the computer.Deprecated[`func SpeechManagerVersion() -> NumVersion`](/documentation/applicationservices/1462334-speechmanagerversion)Determines the current version of the Speech SynthesisManager installed in the system.Deprecated### [Getting Information About Voices](/documentation/applicationservices/speech_synthesis_manager#1657660)

[`func CountVoices(UnsafeMutablePointer<Int16>) -> OSErr`](/documentation/applicationservices/1459947-countvoices)Determines how many voices are available.Deprecated[`func GetIndVoice(Int16, UnsafeMutablePointer<VoiceSpec>) -> OSErr`](/documentation/applicationservices/1464595-getindvoice)Gets a voice specification structure for a voice bypassing an index to the `GetIndVoice` function.Deprecated[`func GetVoiceDescription(UnsafePointer<VoiceSpec>?, UnsafeMutablePointer<VoiceDescription>?, Int) -> OSErr`](/documentation/applicationservices/1463940-getvoicedescription)Gets a description of a voice by using the `GetVoiceDescription` function.Deprecated[`func GetVoiceInfo(UnsafePointer<VoiceSpec>?, OSType, UnsafeMutableRawPointer) -> OSErr`](/documentation/applicationservices/1461410-getvoiceinfo)Gets the same information about a voice that the `GetVoiceDescription` function provides,or to determine in which file and resource a voice is stored. Deprecated[`func MakeVoiceSpec(OSType, OSType, UnsafeMutablePointer<VoiceSpec>) -> OSErr`](/documentation/applicationservices/1461446-makevoicespec)Sets the fields of a voice specification structure. Deprecated### [Starting, Stopping, and Pausing Speech](/documentation/applicationservices/speech_synthesis_manager#1657703)

[`func ContinueSpeech(SpeechChannel) -> OSErr`](/documentation/applicationservices/1462728-continuespeech)Resumes speech paused by the `PauseSpeechAt` function.Deprecated[`func PauseSpeechAt(SpeechChannel, Int32) -> OSErr`](/documentation/applicationservices/1461174-pausespeechat)Pauses speech on a speech channel.Deprecated[`func SpeakCFString(SpeechChannel, CFString, CFDictionary?) -> OSErr`](/documentation/applicationservices/1461621-speakcfstring)Begins speaking a string represented as a `CFString` object.Deprecated[`func StopSpeech(SpeechChannel) -> OSErr`](/documentation/applicationservices/1462745-stopspeech)Terminates speech immediately on the specified channel.Deprecated[`func StopSpeechAt(SpeechChannel, Int32) -> OSErr`](/documentation/applicationservices/1459780-stopspeechat)Terminates speech delivery on a specified channel eitherimmediately or at the end of the current word or sentence.Deprecated### [Registering and Unregistering Synthesizers and Voices](/documentation/applicationservices/speech_synthesis_manager#1680079)

[`func SpeechSynthesisRegisterModuleURL(CFURL) -> OSErr`](/documentation/applicationservices/1459624-speechsynthesisregistermoduleurl)Registers and makes available a speech synthesizer or voice.Deprecated[`func SpeechSynthesisUnregisterModuleURL(CFURL) -> OSErr`](/documentation/applicationservices/1462511-speechsynthesisunregistermoduleu)Unregisters a registered speech synthesizer or voice.Deprecated### [Callbacks](/documentation/applicationservices/speech_synthesis_manager#1657924)

[`typealias SpeechDoneProcPtr`](/documentation/applicationservices/speechdoneprocptr)Defines a pointer to a speech-done callback functionwhich is called when the Speech Synthesis Manager finishes speakinga buffer of text.[`typealias SpeechErrorProcPtr`](/documentation/applicationservices/speecherrorprocptr)Defines a pointer to an error callback functionthat handles syntax errors within commands embedded in a text bufferbeing processed by the Speech Synthesis Manager.[`typealias SpeechErrorCFProcPtr`](/documentation/applicationservices/speecherrorcfprocptr)Defines a pointer to an error callback function that handles syntax errors within commands embedded in a `CFString` object being processed by the Speech Synthesis Manager.[`typealias SpeechPhonemeProcPtr`](/documentation/applicationservices/speechphonemeprocptr)Defines a pointer to a phoneme callback functionthat is called by the Speech Synthesis Manager before it pronouncesa phoneme.[`typealias SpeechSyncProcPtr`](/documentation/applicationservices/speechsyncprocptr)Defines a pointer to a synchronization callbackfunction that is called when the Speech Synthesis Manager encountersa synchronization command embedded in a text buffer.[`typealias SpeechTextDoneProcPtr`](/documentation/applicationservices/speechtextdoneprocptr)Defines a pointer to a text-done callback functionthat is called when the Speech Synthesis Manager has finished processinga buffer of text.[`typealias SpeechWordProcPtr`](/documentation/applicationservices/speechwordprocptr)Defines a pointer to a word callback functionthat is called by the Speech Synthesis Manager before it pronouncesa word.[`typealias SpeechWordCFProcPtr`](/documentation/applicationservices/speechwordcfprocptr)Defines a pointer to a Core Foundation-based word callback function that is called by the Speech Synthesis Manager before it pronounces a word.### [Data Types](/documentation/applicationservices/speech_synthesis_manager#1657990)

the text being synthesized when an error occurs.### [Result Codes](/documentation/applicationservices/speech_synthesis_manager#1659745)

The most common result codes returned by Speech SynthesisManager are listed below.

[`var noSynthFound: Int`](/documentation/coreservices/nosynthfound)Could not find the specified speech synthesizer[`var synthOpenFailed: Int`](/documentation/coreservices/synthopenfailed)Could not open another speech synthesizerchannel[`var synthNotReady: Int`](/documentation/coreservices/synthnotready)Speech synthesizer is still busy speaking[`var bufTooSmall: Int`](/documentation/coreservices/buftoosmall)Output buffer is too small to hold result[`var voiceNotFound: Int`](/documentation/coreservices/voicenotfound)Voice resource not found[`var incompatibleVoice: Int`](/documentation/coreservices/incompatiblevoice)Specified voice cannot be used with synthesizer[`var badDictFormat: Int`](/documentation/coreservices/baddictformat)Pronunciation dictionary format error[`var badInputText: Int`](/documentation/coreservices/badinputtext)Raw phoneme text contains invalid characters## [See Also](/documentation/applicationservices/speech_synthesis_manager#see-also)
